decks.notes: The main focus of this UWx control deck is to disrupt the opponent's game plan through countermagic and removal spells while building card advantage through card draw and library manipulation. The deck aims to control the board state with cards like Teferi, Time Raveler and Swords to Plowshares, while also having a strong late game presence with powerful planeswalkers like Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student. With a mix of counterspells, removal, and card advantage engines, this control deck aims to outvalue the opponent and win the game through incremental advantage.

This deck aims to quickly fill the graveyard with powerful creatures like Griselbrand and Archon of Cruelty, then use reanimation spells like Reanimate and Animate Dead to bring them back onto the battlefield for a game-winning combo. The deck also includes ways to disrupt opponents with cards like Thoughtseize and Cabal Therapy, while using cards like Dark Ritual and Lotus Petal to accelerate its game plan. A versatile mix of disruption, graveyard manipulation, and powerful creatures make this deck a potent force in Legacy 1 vs 1 matches.

The key idea behind this deck is to disrupt the opponent's game plan while applying constant pressure with efficient creatures and control spells. By combining disruptive cards like Thoughtseize, Force of Will, and Daze with aggressive threats like Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student and Nethergoyf, the deck aims to disrupt the opponent's strategy while maintaining a clock to close out the game quickly. The mana denial from cards like Wasteland also adds another layer of disruption, making it difficult for the opponent to develop their board and keep up with the tempo of the Dimir aggro strategy.

The deck focuses on quickly assembling a powerful board presence through the use of artifact creatures and various combos involving graveyard recursion and sacrificing artifacts. By utilizing cards like Emry, Lurker of the Loch, Goblin Engineer, and Mox Opal, the deck aims to generate mana and card advantage while setting up for explosive plays with cards like Agatha's Soul Cauldron and Legion Extruder. The key strategy is to outvalue the opponent by efficiently utilizing resources and assembling key pieces of the combo to overwhelm the opponent before they can respond.

This deck focuses on utilizing graveyard synergy and sacrificing creatures to quickly fill the graveyard and cast Hogaak, Arisen Necropolis. The key strategy is to use cards like Stitcher's Supplier and Thoughtseize to fill the graveyard, while using cards like Wight of the Reliquary and Elvish Reclaimer to take advantage of the graveyard resources. The deck also includes protection and disruption elements, such as Sylvan Safekeeper and Fatal Push, to ensure the combo can be executed smoothly while also disrupting the opponent's game plan.

The main concept of this deck is to quickly bring powerful creatures back from the graveyard and onto the battlefield using spells like Reanimate, Animate Dead, and Shallow Grave. This combo strategy is supported by cards like Unmask and Collective Brutality to disrupt the opponent's game plan and protect your own. By utilizing cards like Archon of Cruelty and Griselbrand, you can put immense pressure on your opponent and close out the game swiftly. The deck also includes Cabal Therapy for additional disruption and card advantage to maintain control of the game.

The main concept of this deck is to quickly and efficiently assemble the combo pieces needed to create a storm of powerful spells with The Fantasticar at its center. By using cards like Dark Ritual and Lotus Petal to generate mana, along with cards like Brainstorm and Thoughtseize to find and protect the combo pieces, the goal is to overwhelm the opponent with a high number of spells in one turn. Cards like Force of Will and Daze provide additional disruption to ensure the combo can go off successfully.

The main concept of this deck is to quickly assemble the Combo pieces, specifically utilizing The Fantasticar and Stormchaser's Talent to generate a powerful board presence. The deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan with cards like Thoughtseize and Force of Will, while also maintaining card advantage with brainstorm and Mishra's Bauble. Using fast mana sources like Lotus Petal and Dark Ritual, the deck can generate a large amount of mana in a short amount of time, allowing for explosive plays and combo finishes. The goal is to outpace and overwhelm the opponent before they are able to establish their own game plan.

The deck focuses on using control elements such as counterspells and removal spells to disrupt the opponent's game plan while efficiently drawing into threats and card advantage to secure the win. The deck aims to control the board state and tempo of the game, using cards like Teferi, Time Raveler and Force of Will to protect its own game plan and creatures like Geist of Saint Traft and Murktide Regent to close out the game. The mana base is optimized for consistency and the deck has versatile answers to a variety of threats.

The key concept of this deck is to use The Fantasticar combo to quickly set up a winning board state. By utilizing cards like Dark Ritual, Lotus Petal, and Lion's Eye Diamond, the deck aims to cast Doomsday to set up a winning sequence of cards in the library, culminating in a Thassa's Oracle win condition. The deck also includes disruptive elements like Force of Will and Thoughtseize to protect the combo and disrupt the opponent's game plan. With careful sequencing and resource management, the deck can achieve a quick and decisive victory.

The deck focuses on utilizing efficient creatures and disruptive spells to maintain tempo and control the game. By combining aggressive threats like Dragon's Rage Channeler and Murktide Regent with cards like Force of Will and Daze, the deck seeks to quickly pressure opponents while disrupting their game plan. The inclusion of cards like Unholy Heat and Lightning Bolt allows for additional removal options to deal with opposing threats. This deck aims to outpace opponents and disrupt their strategy through a combination of aggression and disruption.

This deck focuses on quickly playing aggressive creatures like Delver of Secrets and Dragon's Rage Channeler, while disrupting the opponent's game plan with cards like Force of Will and Wasteland. The goal is to maintain control of the game through tempo plays like Daze and Lightning Bolt, while also utilizing cards like Murktide Regent and Unholy Heat to close out the game quickly. The deck relies on efficient card draw and manipulation with Brainstorm and Ponder to ensure consistency and keep up the pressure on the opponent.

The main concept of this deck is to aggressively control the tempo of the game by utilizing a combination of efficient removal spells like Fatal Push and disruptive cards like Thoughtseize. The deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan while applying pressure with creatures like Murktide Regent and Barrowgoyf. The inclusion of counterspells like Force of Will and Daze helps protect the deck's threats and maintain its aggressive tempo. The deck also benefits from powerful draw and filtering cards like Brainstorm and Ponder to help find the right resources at the right time.

The main concept of this Control - Pox deck is to disrupt the opponent's resources and game plan through cards like Smallpox, Thoughtseize, and Wasteland, while also gaining value from cards like Liliana of the Veil and Karn, the Great Creator. The deck aims to control the board with cards like Ensnaring Bridge and Sheoldred's Edict, while also utilizing Urza's Saga to tutor for key artifacts. With the ability to strip the opponent's hand, disrupt their mana base, and control the board, this deck aims to grind out victories through attrition and resource denial.

This deck focuses on assembling the combo pieces needed to quickly generate mana and disrupt the opponent while setting up for a massive play with The Fantasticar. By using cards like Dark Ritual and Lotus Petal, the deck can power out powerful threats like Murktide Regent and Stormchaser's Talent, enabling explosive plays and overwhelming the opponent. Thoughtseize and Force of Will help to control the game and protect the combo, while cards like Boomerang Basics and Flow State provide additional disruption and tempo advantage.

This deck revolves around quickly generating mana and card advantage using elves, with the ultimate goal of casting Natural Order to search for Craterhoof Behemoth and overwhelm the opponent with a massive army of creatures for a game-ending attack. The deck also includes disruption in the form of Allosaurus Shepherd to protect your combo pieces and Boseiju, Who Endures to ensure your key spells resolve. Glimpse of Nature provides additional card draw to keep the engine running smoothly, allowing you to quickly assemble your combo and overwhelm your opponent.
